data.title: Global Payments partners with UK's Sage for the launch of vendor payments solution
data.description:
Payment technology and software company Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) has partnered with the U.K.-based SME solutions provider Sage (OTCPK:SGGEF) to launch a vendor payments solution.

Vendor Payments is designed for easy, secured, and controlled management of accounts payable across disconnected systems.

data.title: Uber launches options to make it easier for senior citizens to order rides
data.description:
Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) announced on Wednesday the nationwide U.S. rollout of senior accounts. The new feature is aimed at making the company's ride-hailing app more accessible for older adults.

The Uber (NYSE:UBER) initiative introduced a streamlined app experience with larger text, simplified navigation, and fewer buttons, specifically designed to address the needs of seniors. Notably, family members can set up and manage these accounts through Uber's (UBER) family profile, enabling them to request rides, manage payments, and track trips in real time on behalf of their loved ones. 

Seniors can store frequently visited locations for easy booking and choose from flexible payment options, including their own card, a family member’s card, or an eligible Medicare Flex card. Family organizers and senior account holders do not need to live in the same location.

Uber (UBER) also set up options for those who prefer to ride independently. The mobility tech company launched Simple mode, which offers the same simplified interface without requiring a family connection. Both features were developed with input from aging advocates and accessibility experts to promote independence and reduce transportation barriers for older adults. Senior accounts and Simple mode are now available across the U.S. and select international markets, with U.S. users receiving notifications about the new options.

data.title: TotalEnergies expands stake in Brazil's Lapa offshore field in asset swap with Shell
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) said Wednesday it increased its stake in the Lapa offshore field in Brazil to 48% after acquiring a 3% stake from Shell (SHEL), swapping a 20% non-operated interest in the Gato do Mato project with Shell for the added stake in the Lapa field.

Upon closing, TotalEnergies (TOT) will raise its operated stake in Lapa to 48%, alongside Shell (SHEL) with 27% and Repsol (OTCQX:REPYF) (OTCQX:REPYY) Sinopec with 25%.

Lapa is a deep-offshore field that will produce 60K bbl/day by the end of the year.

"This transaction is aligned with our strategy to focus on low-cost, low-emission projects, such as Atapu 2 and Sepia 2 in Brazil, sanctioned in 2024. In addition, it further strengthens our operated position in the Lapa field, in the pre-salt Santos Basin," TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) Senior VP Javier Rielo said.

data.title: Bayer gets FDA nod for Nubeqa in advanced prostate cancer
data.description:
Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) (OTCPK:BAYRY) said on Wednesday that the U.S. FDA has approved its blockbuster oral hormone blocker, darolutamide, in combination therapy, to treat metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer. ((mHSPC)).

Darolutamide, marketed as Nubeqa, is co-developed by Bayer and Orion Corporation, a Finnish pharmaceutical company.

The recent approval follows encouraging results from a late-stage trial, which found that darolutamide combined with androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) decreased the chances of death by 46% when compared to the placebo paired with ADT for patients with mHSPC.

Nubeqa with ADT is now approved in the U.S. for adult patients with mHSPC, with or without docetaxel.

Additionally, Nubeqa is also authorized for adults with non-metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer at high risk for metastasis.

data.title: STMicroelectronics' CEO sees market improving but geopolitical woes loom - report
data.description:
STMicroelectronics' (NYSE:STM) CEO Jean-Marc Chery said the company has seen signs of an upcycle which would boost its results in the coming quarters, unless trade tensions disrupt the positive developments, Reuters reported.

Chery was speaking at an event in Paris hosted by BNP Paribas said the company would deliver at least the mid-point of its second-quarter guidance, the report added.

In its fiscal first quarter results in April, the chipmaker forecast second quarter 2025 net revenue to be $2.71B at midpoint.

STMicroelectronics did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

"The dynamic is pretty interesting. Why? Because quarter-to-date, the book-to-bill ratio is well above parity ... This means you are in a dynamic of upcycle," said Chery.

Chery said this recovery would continue at least until the third quarter, which could have been a turning point for the company's sales to grow year-over-year.

However, the geopolitical scenario, including U.S. President Donald Trump's trade tussle and tariffs, pose an uncertainty over the latter part of 2025, Chery added, according to the report.

Chery noted that the uptick seen so far was not due to the customers overstocking inventories fearing upcoming tariffs.

"We do not see a strong behavior by customers to overstock. I don't say it does not exist, but we don't detect that it exists significantly," Chery added.

In addition, Chery said that the demand remains regionally unbalanced, with the strongest demand being in China and the weakest in Europe, the report added.

data.title: SA Sentiment: Lower odds of a recession after US-China truce
data.description:
After the U.S. and China agreed to roll back tariffs in May, markets are no longer seeing a high risk of a recession happening in the next 12 months.

"The size of the tariff tax hike has been scaled down, imparting less of a purchasing power squeeze," Joseph Lupton, global economist at J.P. Morgan, said recently. "We no longer see a U.S. recession, but expect material headwinds to keep growth weak through the rest of this year."

Beth Ann Bovino, chief economist for U.S. Bank, sees current recession risk at 40%. "If previous tariff levels re-emerge after the 90-day pause, the recession threat is likely to rise again." 

Goldman Sachs economists now see a 35% chance of a recession. "That's still above the usual background risk in any given year of about 15% probability," they noted.

Seeking Alpha readers have also dialed back their expectations for a recession, according to the Sentiment Survey in May that gathered over 1,000 responses.

The percentage of respondents seeing 0%-25% recession odds increased by 11 percentage points M/M. Meanwhile, the percentage of respondents forecasting 50%-75% probability has gone down 8 pp M/M.

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. acquires Wilkins & Associates Insurance Services
Thor Industries posts strong results but sounds caution for upcoming quarters
data.description:
Shares of Thor Industries (NYSE:THO) rose as much as 13% in premarket trading on Wednesday after the RV company reported a strong beat on core metrics in the third quarter and reaffirmed its full-year forecast.

For 2025, the company continues to expect net sales between $9B and $9.5B (midpoint $9.25B, estimate $9.23B) and earnings per share between $3.30 and $4.00 (midpoint $3.65, estimate $3.47). Gross profit margin is expected in the 13.8% to 14.5% range (midpoint 14.3%, estimate 13.9%).

However, CEO Bob Martin warned that the company expects the fourth quarter and the first quarter of fiscal 2026 "to be challenging" as the current economic uncertainty has put "downward pressure on consumer confidence and has negatively impacted retail pull-through." 

In Q3, the company in North America saw towable RV shipments up 5.5% and motorized RV shipments up 10.9%, which offset weakness in the European RV segment, where shipments fell 12.2%.

Gross margin during the quarter was 15.3%, ahead of the 14.7% estimate, which was driven by a 200 bps improvement in its North American towable segment.

Net income for the quarter was $135.2M compared with $114.5M for the same period a year earlier.

On a per-share basis, Thor earned $2.53, ahead of the average analyst estimate by 73 cents.

Revenue rose 3.2% to $2.89B and was ahead of the consensus mark by nearly $300M.

Shares of rivals Winnebago (WGO) and LCI Industries (LCII) were also up between 1% and 2% after Thor's results.

data.title: American Assets Trust forecasts lower 2025 NOI, metric to remain above pre-pandemic level
data.description:
American Assets Trust (NYSE:AAT) said on Wednesday that its 2025 net operating income is projected to decline sequentially, but is still expected to remain above the pre-pandemic level.

Shares were +4.63% pre-market to $21.00.

The metric is expected to fall across the diversified REIT's Office, Retail, and Mixed-use portfolios but is projected to increase across its Multifamily portfolio, an investor presentation showed.

2025 NOI is forecasted to be $262.0M before reserves, compared to $290.1M in 2024. With bad debt credit reserves, NOI is estimated to come at $259.2M, down from $290.1M in 2024.

However, the metric is still expected to remain above the pre-pandemic level. 2019 NOI before reserves and with bad debt reserves stood at $234.7M.

data.title: Yum! Brands gains after Goldman Sachs turns bullish
data.description:
Goldman Sachs upgraded Yum! Brands (NYSE:YUM) to a Buy rating on Wednesday after having the restaurant stock lined up at Neutral. The Taco Bell chain, in particular, was noted to have a record of success in a value-centric environment and was called a highly unique value leader with strong consumer affinity.

Analyst Christine Cho pointed to the restaurant company's best-in-class unit growth trajectory and high franchise mix, which is seen building relative resilience into the business. Yum! Brands (NYSE:YUM) improved digital integration across brands and at the enterprise level were also highlighted as ways the company is improving operational efficiency to drive top-line acceleration. YUM's (YUM) unit growth trajectory was also called out by Cho.

"Management plans to triple Taco Bell’s international store footprint to 3,000+ stores by 2030E from 1,150 in 2024, with 40%+ contribution to new store growth from the brand’s Big 4 markets (UK, Spain, Australia, and India) and with plans to expand into markets like France, Poland, Germany, South Africa, Turkey, UAE, Greece, Ireland, and Sweden."

Goldman Sachs assigned a price target of $167 to Yum! Brands (YUM).

Shares of Yum! Brands (YUM) were up 1.4% in premarket trading.
